pattern: Each row's seating is a bitmask, and because cheating only reaches the row directly above, a row-by-row dynamic program over compatible masks finds the maximum seated students.
hints: Cheating only looks sideways and diagonally up, never straight ahead or behind, so a row's validity depends only on itself and the row immediately above it.
Represent who sits in a row as a bitmask. A mask is self-valid if no two chosen seats are adjacent and every chosen seat is on a good seat.

Carry, per row, the best total achievable for each valid mask. A row mask is compatible with the previous row's mask when no seat is diagonally adjacent (the left/right shifts must not overlap). Sum popcounts across rows, maximising over compatible transitions.

edge_inputs.description: scattered broken seats forcing diagonal awareness
checkerboard of broken seats allows packing alternate columns
single fully broken row seats nobody
one open row of three forces a gap between students
two adjacent open rows where diagonal conflicts dominate

stress.description: random 8x8 boards with a mix of broken and good seats
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 8)
    n = rng.randint(1, 8)
    return [[['#' if rng.random() < 0.35 else '.' for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]]
stress.note: small dimensions keep the 2^n mask DP fast even at the cap
